title: helleborine palustris var. ericetorum
intro: Helleborine palustris var. ericetorum, also known as the marsh helleborine, is a rare and protected subspecies of orchid that grows in wetland areas in Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, fever, and other infections. The health benefits of marsh helleborine include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and act as an antioxidant, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
